name: Arashi: Castles of Sin - Final Cut

reviews.review_description: Yes it is a little bit glitch and the graphics aren't the best but it does get fun at times like there's so many weapons and you really feel like a never seen blood splashing samurai assassin like you can't tell me jumping from a house coming down with two swords at a enemy is super cool and the first one that came out was a huge success so if you think about it the game isn't bad its the version and don't forget this game is 22.99 I think and was made by sky dance the same studio that made other award winning awesome games so if it was loved by many it would be an amazing game so if you really want it then get it im not gonna stop you but don't start crying if it has two glitches.

I’m seeing a lot of mixed reviews here, and I think people are missing the context. You have to realize that Arashi is a port of a 2021 PSVR title, not a game built from the ground up for 2025.
If you come into this expecting the physics of Blade & Sorcery or the complexity of newer titles, you’re setting yourself up for disappointment. But if you judge it for what it actually is—a remaster of a classic VR stealth game—it’s fantastic. It’s basically a VR tribute to Tenchu with arcade-style mechanics that fit the era it was made in.
One critical tip regarding the controls: Commanding Haru (the wolf) is based on head tracking (gaze), not your controller pointer. You have to actually LOOK at the enemy or spot you want him to attack, then press the button. A lot of people try to point with their hand and think it’s broken, but it works perfectly once you realize you aim with your headset.
The "Final Cut" update did a great job sharpening the visuals for the Quest 3, but the core game is still that 2021 experience. If you want a fun ninja simulator and respect its age, this is an easy 5 stars.
Game has a few issues with the dog commands….the graphics are very good,the game play is okay ,cannot understand all the harsh reviews, personally feel this was a real bargain at sale price £7.99

The game is completely unfinished and rough. I wish I could’ve refunded it, but I bought is on the 2nd of this month, and didn’t have the chance to play it until just now. And it was awful. Plus I cannot refund it due to not being able to immediately play it.

Graphics: mid. 

Animations: non existent. 

Physics: bad. 

Combat: not fun.

Collision with objects: practically non existent.

Hit detection on enemies: terrible.

Sound: repetitive AF.

Gameplay: really not that great apart from being able to throw ball for and pet doggo. 

I wish I didn’t buy this game.

*GFX: 7.1 outta 10*
*Sound: 8.1 outta 10*
*Gameplay (Dual swords, Shuriken & other weapons): 8.6 outta 10*
*Fun Level: 7.8 outta 10*

I didn't know what to expect with all of the good & bad reviews but after playing for 2 hours I was suprised to find a nice core game with a cool martial arts theme. The "Nito-ryu" (two swords) combat was interesting and the attack and defense movement was fluid. The game left me wanting more but the level design was a bit lacking. The night shinobi atmosphere was cool as well. And of course, sword duels under the moonlight.
<<Overall: 7.9 outta 10>>
Best game i have ever played

I had high hopes for this game but it was wayyyyy too easy. If it was perfected and made a bit more difficult then it would have been a 5 star. There is literally little to no use for any of the in game items because you can casually just fight your way through almost all the game and sneaking is so easy and it is actually quite difficult to get caught
I want to be physics based like blade and sorcery ￼
It could be a good game, but unfortunately, it's a terrible game.

I played it on quest 3. 
It can't be compared to an assassin's creed or any new game. It's a port of an old game and really feels like one. The high rating comes from the fact that I like this kind of games (and we don't have many of them in VR).

The good :

The game has a story and a good art style, but doesn't match the trailer nor pictures (they probably comes from the PC version).

The bad :

The anti-aliasing is really strong and the resolution really low. Everything is shimmering. 
The texture aren't that great either.

The combat is okay at best. During a fight, it's strange that enemies are mostly invincible (even if you hit their back) except right after a pary (and sometimes,  right after or before an attack). There is no clue to tell you when an enemy is vulnerable (aside from successful pary). You have 2 swords but I still don't understand the use of the short one. Lastly, you can't block an attack by holding your weapon motionless (the enemy sword will pass through yours most of the times). You can only pary (actively hit the enemies weapon as they try to hit you).

The good news is, you don't really need to fight (except the boss). The enemies are near sighted, partially blind, mostly deaf, never call for help and get "one shot" by any hit when not in "combat mode". Most enemies aren't even looking in your direction as you approach them. 
You also have plenty of ammunition that can "one shot" most enemies (even in "combat mode") and a dog that can hold them for an easy kill. 

The slow motion on every kill is really annoying and slow you down unnecessarily. 

Lastly the difficulty spikes are huge. Until you get to the castle where your dog can't enter, everything is a breeze (and boring). In the castle, it's still mostly easy (just one shot everyone in the back). 
But when the boss appears, you suddenly switch to hard mode.

The game crash at the first cutscene after the first boss (out of memory).

This company has made VR games before, have they not? How can you possibly have this game ready for preorder, for as long as it has been, and yet still release a VR game, where you have to fight with the controls to move yourself. This is bloody horrible. Where is my option for movement where I’m facing. This is VR. If it’s not out like within this week, I’ll be refunding this. Get people excited, and yet release a unplayable game, to VR veterans, you are trying to walk forward where you are looking, and you push the analog up, it goes sideways. Absolutely horrible. So they just released the crappy PSVR version on here. So you have to face the right way all the time. I think with laziness I’m refunding. 
      Yes this game looks awesome, looks like it’ll be a lot of fun, and I will change this if it gets fixed. However; I won’t be keeping it, if it’s not, therefore I won’t be able to change this. So you’ll know one way or another.

reviews.review_title: delight for Shinobi and Tenchu fans
reviews.review_description:
one of the best action-adventure solo campaigns in VR, true to those classics.

as usual, don't pay much attention to bad reviews by "VR community" cry babies. Whenever any actual full game comes to VR, minigame and tech demo shills rush in with bad takes. The game was released with a bug involving smooth turning. This has been fixed in update, their ridiculous 1 star scores have not.

this game was awesome on psvr, and the port is quite good on Quest 2  updates brought better resolution and the control problems with smooth turning have been fixed. I love the sights and sounds of feudal Japan brought back to life in VR with expansive environments with plenty of options for traversal and exploration.

melee swordfighting is like AC Nexus or Until you Fall, all about timing and direction. But more challenging as it's about precise parrying and counterattack rather than mere blocking. Dodging is an option as is fleeing from fight when outnumbered. It's mostly about stealth with ninjas. Trust your bow, shurikens, bombs, blowguns and trusty wolf and attack from shadows, but boss battles are unavoidable.

finished yesterday. I recommend it heartily. Is it a 5 stars like AW2? no, much smaller and less polished, but good for a ninja adventure in feudal Japan unlike anywhere else in VR.

There is no excuse for this in 2023. A Beta release at best. Hellsweeper and Assassins Creed run circles around this. Any of the Team Beef ports run circles around this. 100% Jive. No bueno. Refunded after 10mins 👎

Excpected a fun samurai stealth game, got a low res dissapointment with terrible combat and movement. Just don't go through all of the effort to buy this game like I did, you're gonna end up refunding it.

I had to agree with everyone they need to updated this game for real I don’t even understand where we started at where we at now pass through blaze is crazy dog get you caught the direction is retarded. Lotta us is gamers we use analog in our body to move in every game should be just like that I thought it was gonna be my most dissipated title I was wrong Arizona sunshine. I wish it were longer, but they killed the dog, the best pet ever. But not gonna talk about another game. Maybe you should call them ask them what they did to make. The gameplay is terrible 
The movement is terrible

To expensive for a piece of crap
It doesn’t even deserve on star

Stealth in feudal japan my dream. Tenchu in vr. My dream. Great stealth. Great game. AND AT LAST NO MORE PERMANENT AUTOMATIC TAGGING. thank you so much. Alerted yiu have an icon no more alerted no more icon therefore you do not where the bad guys are. Which is adamant to keep the fun....Finally was that too hard assasin creed nexus or vampire masquerade?.  If that game had permanent AUTOMATIC tagging i would have asked a refund.....the best option would be to Give us the choice....say you press on b you to yourself auto tagg ......great game. A must buy ...MUST BUY......well done.....quest 2 or 3 is not made for kids.....do not treat as such......thank you so much for this game.......long live arashi...
And long live meta......also slight bug when you physically crouch...also if you use snap turning while crouching the floor level of your guardian is off and you are suddenly a giant...not good to  stay stealthy.and healthy...if you  do not use the snap.turn of the right joystick you can bypass this problem.

This is the kind of rushed rubbish that can give the Quest platform a bad reputation.

I was so looking forward to this release, only to find it completely misses the mark.
Reminds me of the abysmal Grid Legends release from EA.

META should not allow this on their store!

Compare this to the amazing Assassins Creed Nexus, and you have one of the best and one of the worst releases this year.

reviews.review_title: A lesson in how not to do things
reviews.review_description:
The controls are horrible, when you exit the combat many times it happens that when you press forward it goes backwards.
Textures worthy of quest 1, 0 interaction with the environment, repetitive combat, invisible edges with little taste.
I hope the developers will listen to the community and make future improvements and fixes.
there is still time to fix it.

The graphics I can get behind, but the fixed movement really doesn't sell it. There is potential that they could fix and polish the game further, but at this point, I'm not really counting on it.

And you always hold the weapons, if your not going to make big changes at lest make it where you can put the weapons where you like . And for others move how they like. Not sure why games make you move with only the right or left hand needs to be both
Fun to play, and worth buying. Graphics and sword fighting could be better. Other than that, still entertaining!

reviews.review_title: Read this if struggling with movement
reviews.review_description:
Even though this game is meant to be a remastered version, it still feels dated in many ways. When smooth turning is enabled, you only walk in one direction, which should be a separate option. Luckily you can just leave snap turning on for head based movement.

Swords clip through enemies on death and they they do a very basic death animation (no hacked limbs sorry folks!). Also doesn't feel particularly optimized for quest 3, but it's a good looking enough game. 

Need to spend more time in this game, but wanted to put this review out for first impression feedback and also to help those struggling with the movement/turn situation (as I did for the first few minutes).
